title: AI client auto-config — configure + detect across a sample of MCP clients

steps.title: Prerequisites
steps.body: The Hub app running. At least two AI clients installed from the supported set (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, Roo Code, Gemini CLI, etc.). The skills/client-paths.json manifest is present (the canonical client catalog + skill install paths + mcpClientMapping). This scenario validates that the Hub auto-configures the MCP server connection + skill install path for each detected client, and that the configuration is correct (right command, right project path, right skill directory).

steps.title: Open the AI client configuration panel
steps.body: In the Hub app, open the project and navigate to the AI Client configuration panel. The panel should show a list of detected clients (those installed on this machine). For each, a 'Configure' button writes the MCP server config + skill path.

steps.title: Expected outcomes (client detection)
items: The panel lists at least the clients installed on this machine (e.g. Claude Code, Cursor)
Each detected client shows its skill install path (from skills/client-paths.json)
Each detected client shows the MCP server command it will use (node mcp-server/dist/index.js with UNITY_PROJECT_PATH env)
Clients not installed are listed but greyed out / marked 'not detected'

steps.title: Configure one client (Claude Code) and verify the config file
Expected outcomes (config + skill written)
items: generate_skill writes SKILL.md to the Claude skill directory (from skills/client-paths.json)
The MCP server config is written to the Claude config file (e.g. ~/.claude/claude_desktop_config.json or the project .mcp.json)
The config entry uses the correct command (node), the correct server path (mcp-server/dist/index.js), and sets UNITY_PROJECT_PATH to the project's absolute path
The config entry includes the bridge port env var OR relies on instance discovery (no hardcoded wrong port)

steps.title: Verify the manifest covers the expected client set
Expected outcomes (manifest coverage)
items: client-paths.json lists ≥13 skill-target clients (the full supported set)
The mcpClientMapping section covers ≥14 agent variants (the expanded McpClientId enum)
Each client entry has a skillInstallPath (or skill directory) + an mcpConfigPath
No client entry references an internal specs/ path or milestone ID

steps.title: Remove the generated skill (config files are left for the operator to inspect)
Paste the agent output (generate_skill + script_read envelopes) + the Hub panel screenshot

reset.note: Optional scenario. The client detection + config-write is Hub UI; the skill-write + manifest-verify is agent-driven. The skills/client-paths.json manifest landed in Plan 5 (folded into the existing manifest, not a separate hub/client-catalog.json). Covers a sample of clients — full 14+ client matrix is the Plan 5 EditMode test suite.
